Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and a low HbA1c. These findings suggest impaired renal function (possible acute or chronic kidney disease) and abnormal urinary findings, but no evidence of current hyperglycemia or diabetes. Urine appearance and color are abnormal, raising suspicion for possible urinary tract or hepatic involvement. Clinical correlation and further nephrological and hepatic evaluation are recommended.
